steps.title: Join two clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: This idea is known as the service recovery paradox and research suggests that it has important implications. Standard punctuation requires a period, semicolon, or comma plus coordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Reject faulty joins
steps.content: A creates a comma splice, B creates a fused sentence, and D places the comma after the conjunction instead of before it. The comma-plus-and pattern is the correct join. The answer is C.
